data.title: Whales Shift Strategies as Ethereum Sees Price Surge
data.description:
According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, recent monitoring by HyperInsight indicates a significant shift in trading strategies among major cryptocurrency holders, or 'whales,' following a notable increase in Ethereum's (ETH) price to approximately $3,890. This price movement appears to have prompted a change in support and resistance levels, leading several whales to close short positions across multiple cryptocurrencies.

One whale, identified by the address starting with 0x7a9, closed short positions in ASTER and Bitcoin (BTC), reducing their nominal position value by nearly $15 million. The funds were then redirected to open a long position in ETH with a 20x leverage at an average price of $3,938, resulting in a current nominal position value of $12.12 million.

Another whale, with an address beginning with 0x19a, closed a BTC short position, decreasing their nominal position value by approximately $10 million. This capital was used to establish a long position in ETH with a 6x leverage at an average price of $3,939, bringing the current nominal position value to $9.09 million.

A third whale, identified by the address starting with 0x960, closed short positions in BTC and HYPE, reducing their nominal position value by about $2.6 million. The funds were then allocated to open a long position in ETH with a 15x leverage at an average price of $3,950, resulting in a current nominal position value of $9.07 million.

These strategic shifts highlight the dynamic nature of cryptocurrency trading, particularly among large-scale investors, as they respond to market fluctuations and adjust their positions accordingly.

data.title: Matrixport: Bitcoin fell below the bull-bear dividing line for two consecutive weeks, and capital inflows weakened
data.description: Matrixport released its market outlook, stating, "Bitcoin's recent price action suggests the market is transitioning from a bull market to a consolidation phase. While the macroeconomic environment remains supportive, including continued easing by the Federal Reserve and a relatively stable overall liquidity environment, several technical and structural indicators suggest short-term weakness. Bitcoin has fallen below its 21-week moving average for two consecutive weeks, historically considered a reliable demarcation between bull and bear markets. On-chain liquidity growth has also slowed, and realized market capitalization indicators suggest weakening capital inflows. The October 11th flash crash highlighted these vulnerabilities—billions of dollars in forced liquidations triggered a chain reaction in the market. Bitcoin open interest declined, long-term holders began to take profits, and volatility remained low. The interplay of a stronger dollar, falling bond yields, and weak labor market data suggests a potential slowdown in global growth momentum, which is likely to cause risk assets, including Bitcoin, to remain range-bound until market confidence is restored."

data.title: Story Ecosystem IPRWA Agreement Aria and Sedition Join Forces to Enter the Digital Art IP Field
data.description: Aria has announced a partnership with digital art platform Sedition, officially expanding into the visual arts IP market. The two parties will launch a Visual Art Grant Program. Commissioned artworks will be showcased on Sedition, and streaming revenue rights will be authenticated and tokenized on-chain through Aria. Royalties will be shared between artists and Aria IP RWA holders. Furthermore, Aria is about to hold a Time-to-Growth (TGE) and has announced an airdrop plan, allocating 10% of its native token, ARIAIP, to the community.

data.title: Bitcoin's Volatility and Potential for Significant Decline Discussed by Experts
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitcoin's inherent volatility remains a topic of concern, with BitMine chair Tom Lee cautioning that the cryptocurrency could potentially lose up to half its value under certain conditions. In a recent interview with crypto entrepreneur Anthony Pompliano, Lee expressed his belief that Bitcoin is still susceptible to significant drawdowns, stating, "I’m sure there will be 50% drawdowns." This perspective challenges the growing sentiment among market participants who argue that Bitcoin's volatility may decrease as spot Bitcoin ETFs and institutional interest contribute to market stability.

Lee contends that Bitcoin continues to mirror the stock market's movements, often amplifying them. He noted that the stock market has experienced frequent 25% drawdowns, highlighting the progress made over the past six years despite these fluctuations. "The stock market has more frequent 25% drawdowns," Lee explained. "So if the S&P is down 20, Bitcoin could be down 40." Furthermore, Lee suggested that Bitcoin may be transitioning from its traditional four-year cycle, which would have indicated a peak in October, to a "longer cycle" that is now developing.

In a separate discussion on the Bankless podcast, Lee reiterated his optimistic forecast for Bitcoin, predicting it could reach $200,000 to $250,000 by the end of the year. A 50% correction from such levels would bring Bitcoin's price down to approximately $125,000, close to its current all-time high. However, if Bitcoin has already peaked, as some proponents of the four-year cycle suggest, a 50% decline from its current price of $109,981 would result in a value of around $54,990, a level not seen since September 2024, according to CoinMarketCap.

Veteran trader Peter Brandt has echoed similar bearish sentiments, comparing Bitcoin's price chart to the soybean market in the 1970s, which experienced a 50% decline. Historical data supports the possibility of such steep declines, as seen in November 2021 when Bitcoin reached a record high of $69,000 before plummeting approximately 50% to around $35,000 by late January 2022. Despite these concerns, some Bitcoin advocates remain optimistic about the future. Strategy chairman Michael Saylor, for instance, expressed confidence in June, stating, "Winter is not coming back."

data.title: Solo Bitcoin Miner Wins Block Reward, Boosting Decentralization
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, a solo Bitcoin miner has recently achieved a significant milestone by winning a block reward worth $347,455, often referred to as the 'Bitcoin mining lottery.' This achievement was facilitated through the Public Pool Bitcoin mining pool, where the miner earned a 3.125 Bitcoin (BTC) block reward along with an additional 0.016 BTC transaction fee. The event occurred at block height 920,440 on Thursday at 7:32 pm UTC, as indicated by data from Mempool.space.

Solo Bitcoin miners occasionally secure blocks, but this instance stands out due to the miner's ability to accomplish the feat independently by operating a solo mining pool. This approach contrasts with the more prevalent method of pooling hash power with other miners. Umbrel, a Bitcoin node infrastructure company, emphasized the miner's self-sovereignty, stating, 'No middlemen. No third-parties. Just pure self-sovereignty in action.' The Bitcoin Bazaar X account echoed this sentiment, highlighting the miner's total sovereignty achieved by hosting their own mining pool on an Umbrel Server.

The rise in solo Bitcoin miners successfully solving blocks is seen as a positive development for Bitcoin's decentralization. It provides smaller miners with a better opportunity to compete against large industrial-scale miners, many of which are publicly traded entities. This trend coincides with an increase in smaller-sized Bitcoin miners, such as Bitaxes, which have gained popularity in recent years. These miners are available at prices ranging from $155 to over $600, depending on their terahash-per-second capacity.

Despite their relatively modest contribution to Bitcoin's overall hash rate, these pocket-sized machines have been open-sourced to counter the 'secrecy and exclusivity' associated with larger Bitcoin miners, which typically utilize closed-sourced Bitcoin ASICs. A spokesperson from BitMaker shared this perspective with Cointelegraph in 2023, emphasizing the importance of open-sourcing to promote transparency and inclusivity within the Bitcoin mining community.

data.title: Gate launches Orochi Network (ON)
data.description: According to an official announcement, Gate will launch live trading of Orochi Network (ON) perpetual contracts (settled in USDT) at 16:30 (UTC+8) on October 24th, supporting 1-20x leverage. The Gate Perp DEX will also be launched simultaneously. ON margin lending trading will open at 18:10 (UTC+8) on October 24th. ON trading bots and copy trading will be available within one hour of the perpetual contract launch. ON flash swap and fixed investment features will be available one hour after spot trading begins.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $20.3254 million yesterday, with BlackRock IBIT leading the way with a net inflow of $108 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $20.3254 million yesterday (October 23rd, US Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest daily net inflow yesterday was Blackrock's IBIT ETF, with a net inflow of $108 million, bringing IBIT's total net inflow to $65.273 billion. The second largest daily net inflow was Bitwise ETF BITB, with a net inflow of $17.4081 million, bringing BITB's total net inflow to $2.394 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest daily net outflow yesterday was Grayscale's GBTC ETF, with a net outflow of $60.4850 million, bringing GBTC's total net outflow to $24.616 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Bitcoin spot ETF was US$149.431 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market value as a percentage of the total market value of Bitcoin) reached 6.84%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$61.894 billion.

data.title: Only 274 addresses successfully participated in the Stable pre-deposit activity
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, only 274 addresses successfully participated in the $825 million pre-deposit campaign for the public stablecoin Stable. A single whale/institution secured $500 million through nine addresses, representing a 60.6% share.
